lyrics:
Hither come softly into the day
Where dreams leave fingerprints fading away
Breathe through the ache of the waking trees
Weave your lost longing back through the breeze

Hither come softly into the light
Shadow runs sharper just out of sight
Name every sorrow that wears your name
Kiss every fracture you tried to blame

Hither come softly remembering this
The soul’s first scar is the soul’s first kiss
Draw toward the cracks we miss-
And hither come softly into the bliss

Hither come softly into the dusk
Cradle the ember inside the husk
Sing to the silence that knows your voice
Anchor your falling - and make it choice

Hither come softly into the night
Dance with the sorrow and call it right
Float through the deep where the broken sleep
Dream yourself whole, and fall into sleep

Hither come softly remembering this
The wound you bear is the door you kiss
Draw toward the grace we miss-
And hither come softly into the bliss

We beckon the life we fear to see
Not knowing it always beckoned me
Come hither softly, eternally

Hither come softly remembering this
Not all that's lost was meant to exist
Draw toward the breath we miss-
And hither come softly into the bliss
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track creates a deeply introspective and melancholic atmosphere, building from sparse, clean verses into dense, cathartic choruses. The energy level shifts dramatically, supported by a modern production where punchy drums and wide guitars create a powerful foundation without overwhelming the clear, present lead vocal. The arrangement feels meticulously crafted, using space and layers to guide the listener through a journey of reflection and emotional release. Its most defining quality is this powerful dynamic lift into the chorus, creating an anthemic sense of arrival that feels both vulnerable and resilient.

review.review_text: "The soul's first scar is the soul's first kiss" lands the way the best chorus lines do: you hear it once and feel like you already knew it. In C# minor and moving at a crisp 123 BPM, the track earns its anthemic lift the honest way, building from spare, clean verses into a wide, punchy chorus that feels like relief rather than bombast. It's the kind of song that reminds you of early Coldplay at their most direct, before the production got too tidy. A highlight.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'The soul's first scar is the soul's first kiss'
dynamic build from sparse verse to chorus
